title: ajuga zakhoensis
intro: Ajuga zakhoensis, also known as Zakho bugleweed, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of the Middle East and South Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Ajuga zakhoensis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are believed to have health benefits such as reducing stress, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
